courses.title: Advanced Computer Science
courses.description: An upper-level foundations course that develops rigorous competency in data structures, algorithm design, recursive problem solving, and hierarchical tree-based systems.

modules.title: Data Structures
modules.description: Analyzes linear and associative data representations, their implementation trade-offs, and their role in efficient program design.

lessons.title: Abstract Data Types and Complexity Contracts
lessons.description: Defines abstract data types, representation invariants, and operation-level complexity guarantees.

lessons.title: Linked Structures and Dynamic Memory
lessons.description: Implements singly linked lists, doubly linked lists, stacks, and queues while reasoning about references and memory ownership.

lessons.title: Hash Tables and Collision Resolution
lessons.description: Compares chaining and open addressing strategies and evaluates load-factor effects on lookup performance.

learning_outcomes.statement: Select an appropriate abstract data type by comparing operation costs and representation constraints.
Implement and test a dynamic data structure while preserving its representation invariants.

modules.title: Algorithms
modules.description: Develops formal methods for designing, analyzing, and validating efficient algorithms over structured data.

lessons.title: Asymptotic Analysis
lessons.description: Uses Big-O, Big-Theta, and Big-Omega notation to compare algorithmic growth rates and scalability.

lessons.title: Divide-and-Conquer Design
lessons.description: Constructs recursive divide-and-conquer algorithms and derives recurrence relations for their running times.

lessons.title: Sorting and Search Strategy Trade-offs
lessons.description: Evaluates comparison-based sorting, binary search, stability, and the impact of input assumptions.

learning_outcomes.statement: Analyze an algorithm's time and space complexity using asymptotic notation.
Design a divide-and-conquer solution and justify its correctness with a recurrence-based analysis.

modules.title: Trees
modules.description: Introduces hierarchical data structures, traversal algorithms, balanced search trees, and recursive tree reasoning.

lessons.title: Tree Terminology and Recursive Representation
lessons.description: Models rooted trees using node, edge, depth, height, and subtree concepts with recursive definitions.

lessons.title: Binary Search Tree Operations
lessons.description: Implements insertion, search, deletion, and traversal operations while maintaining binary search tree ordering.

lessons.title: Balancing and Tree Performance
lessons.description: Explains how AVL-style balancing controls height and preserves efficient tree operations.

learning_outcomes.statement: Trace recursive traversals and explain how subtree structure determines traversal output.
Implement binary search tree operations and evaluate their performance under balanced and unbalanced inputs.

modules.title: Recursion
modules.description: Builds fluency with recursive specifications, call-stack behavior, termination arguments, and recursive decomposition.

lessons.title: Base Cases and Recursive Decomposition
lessons.description: Constructs recursive functions by identifying base cases, smaller subproblems, and progress toward termination.

lessons.title: The Call Stack and Recursive State
lessons.description: Traces activation records, parameter bindings, and return values during nested recursive calls.

lessons.title: Recursive Backtracking
lessons.description: Applies recursive search to constrained problems such as permutations, subsets, and path exploration.

learning_outcomes.statement: Write a terminating recursive function with correct base cases and a valid recursive reduction.
Use call-stack traces to diagnose incorrect recursive state and backtracking behavior.

courses.title: Web Development Fundamentals
courses.description: A comprehensive introduction to modern web development covering frontend frameworks, backend APIs, and full-stack deployment strategies.

modules.title: HTML & Semantic Markup
modules.description: Covers semantic HTML5, accessibility standards, and document structure for modern web applications.

lessons.title: Semantic Elements and Document Structure
lessons.description: Uses header, nav, main, article, and footer elements to create meaningful document hierarchies.

lessons.title: Forms and User Input
lessons.description: Builds accessible forms with validation, input types, and proper labeling for screen readers.

lessons.title: Accessibility and ARIA
lessons.description: Implements WCAG 2.1 guidelines using ARIA roles, states, and properties for inclusive design.

learning_outcomes.statement: Create semantic HTML documents that pass accessibility audits and validate against W3C standards.
Build forms with client-side validation that degrade gracefully without JavaScript.

modules.title: CSS & Responsive Design
modules.description: Explores modern CSS layout systems, responsive design patterns, and component-based styling.

lessons.title: Flexbox and Grid Layouts
lessons.description: Constructs flexible and grid-based layouts using modern CSS properties and alignment techniques.

lessons.title: Media Queries and Mobile-First Design
lessons.description: Implements responsive breakpoints and mobile-first strategies for cross-device compatibility.

lessons.title: CSS Custom Properties and Theming
lessons.description: Uses CSS variables and custom properties to create maintainable, themeable design systems.

learning_outcomes.statement: Design responsive layouts using Flexbox and Grid that adapt to viewport sizes from 320px to 4K.
Implement a design token system using CSS custom properties for consistent theming.

modules.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
modules.description: Covers modern JavaScript including ES6+ features, asynchronous programming, and DOM manipulation.

lessons.title: ES6+ Features and Modern Syntax
lessons.description: Uses destructuring, spread operators, arrow functions, and modules in contemporary JavaScript code.

lessons.title: Asynchronous JavaScript and Promises
lessons.description: Handles asynchronous operations using Promises, async/await, and error handling patterns.

lessons.title: DOM Manipulation and Events
lessons.description: Manipulates the Document Object Model and handles user interactions with event delegation.

learning_outcomes.statement: Write modern JavaScript using ES6+ features and module systems for maintainable code.
Implement asynchronous workflows using Promises and async/await with proper error handling.

modules.title: React & Component Architecture
modules.description: Introduces React fundamentals, component composition, state management, and hooks.

lessons.title: Components, Props, and JSX
lessons.description: Builds reusable React components using functional components, props, and JSX syntax.

lessons.title: State Management with Hooks
lessons.description: Manages component state using useState, useEffect, and custom hooks for side effects.

lessons.title: Context API and Global State
lessons.description: Shares state across component trees using Context API and reduces prop drilling.

learning_outcomes.statement: Design component hierarchies with clear prop interfaces and composition patterns.
Implement state management using React hooks and Context for scalable applications.

modules.title: Backend APIs & Deployment
modules.description: Covers RESTful API design, database integration, and modern deployment strategies.

lessons.title: RESTful API Design
lessons.description: Designs RESTful endpoints using proper HTTP methods, status codes, and resource naming.

lessons.title: Database Integration with ORMs
lessons.description: Connects APIs to databases using ORMs, migrations, and query optimization techniques.

lessons.title: Deployment and CI/CD
lessons.description: Deploys full-stack applications using containerization, cloud platforms, and automated pipelines.

learning_outcomes.statement: Design and implement RESTful APIs following REST conventions and OpenAPI specifications.
Deploy containerized applications with automated CI/CD pipelines to cloud platforms.

courses.title: Data Science & Analytics
courses.description: A practical introduction to data science covering statistical analysis, machine learning fundamentals, and data visualization.

modules.title: Python for Data Science
modules.description: Covers Python programming fundamentals and essential libraries for data manipulation and analysis.

lessons.title: NumPy and Array Operations
lessons.description: Performs numerical computations using NumPy arrays, broadcasting, and vectorized operations.

lessons.title: Pandas DataFrames
lessons.description: Manipulates structured data using Pandas DataFrames, Series, and data cleaning techniques.

lessons.title: Data Import and Export
lessons.description: Reads and writes data from CSV, JSON, Excel, and SQL databases using Pandas and specialized libraries.

learning_outcomes.statement: Perform efficient numerical computations using NumPy arrays and vectorized operations.
Clean, transform, and analyze datasets using Pandas DataFrames and built-in methods.

modules.title: Statistical Analysis
modules.description: Introduces descriptive and inferential statistics, hypothesis testing, and probability distributions.

lessons.title: Descriptive Statistics and Distributions
lessons.description: Calculates measures of central tendency, dispersion, and visualizes probability distributions.

lessons.title: Hypothesis Testing and p-values
lessons.description: Conducts t-tests, chi-square tests, and ANOVA to evaluate statistical significance.

lessons.title: Correlation and Regression
lessons.description: Analyzes relationships between variables using correlation coefficients and linear regression models.

learning_outcomes.statement: Apply descriptive statistics to summarize datasets and identify distribution characteristics.
Design and interpret hypothesis tests with proper p-value analysis and confidence intervals.

modules.title: Data Visualization
modules.description: Covers principles of effective visualization and hands-on implementation using modern libraries.

lessons.title: Visualization Principles and Design
lessons.description: Applies Tufte's principles, color theory, and Gestalt laws to create effective visualizations.

lessons.title: Matplotlib and Seaborn
lessons.description: Creates static visualizations using Matplotlib and statistical plots using Seaborn.

lessons.title: Interactive Dashboards with Plotly
lessons.description: Builds interactive dashboards and web-based visualizations using Plotly and Dash.

learning_outcomes.statement: Design visualizations following best practices for clarity, accuracy, and aesthetic appeal.
Create interactive dashboards that enable exploratory data analysis and stakeholder communication.

modules.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals
modules.description: Introduces supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, model evaluation, and practical implementation.

lessons.title: Supervised Learning and Classification
lessons.description: Implements classification algorithms including logistic regression, decision trees, and random forests.

lessons.title: Regression and Prediction
lessons.description: Builds regression models for continuous prediction tasks and evaluates model performance.

lessons.title: Model Evaluation and Validation
lessons.description: Uses cross-validation, confusion matrices, ROC curves, and metrics to assess model quality.

learning_outcomes.statement: Select and implement appropriate machine learning algorithms for classification and regression tasks.
Evaluate model performance using appropriate metrics and validation strategies to prevent overfitting.

proposed_changes.summary: Reorder the Recursion module ahead of Trees so learners establish recursive reasoning and call-stack fluency before applying those ideas to tree traversal and binary search tree operations.
affected_items: Recursion: The Call Stack and Recursive State lesson
Trees: Tree Terminology and Recursive Representation lesson
Trees: Binary Search Tree Operations lesson
Quiz 3: Recursive Tree Traversal and BST Invariants
proposed_changes.generated_diff:
--- curriculum/module-order
+++ curriculum/module-order
@@
- 3. Trees
- 4. Recursion
+ 3. Recursion
+ 4. Trees
@@
- Trees: Tree Terminology and Recursive Representation
+  Prerequisite added: Recursion: The Call Stack and Recursive State
+ Trees: Tree Terminology and Recursive Representation
@@
- Quiz 3 assesses traversal after the Trees module
+ Quiz 3 moves after Trees and now assumes completion of the Recursion module

proposed_changes.summary: Add English-language learner accommodations to all Data Structures lessons, including pre-taught terminology, visual process diagrams, and sentence frames for algorithm explanations.
affected_items: Abstract Data Types and Complexity Contracts lesson
Linked Structures and Dynamic Memory lesson
Hash Tables and Collision Resolution lesson
proposed_changes.generated_diff:
--- curriculum/modules/data-structures/metadata
+++ curriculum/modules/data-structures/metadata
@@
+ accessibility.esl_support: true
+ accessibility.glossary_terms: [abstract data type, invariant, pointer, collision]
+ accessibility.visual_aids: [operation-cost table, linked-list diagram, hash-collision animation]
+ accessibility.sentence_frames: ["The operation is efficient because...", "This structure preserves..."]

proposed_changes.user_prompt: Remove Variables as a prerequisite for Functions
proposed_changes.summary: Remove the Variables prerequisite from Functions, but flag the resulting gaps in parameter binding, local state, recursive activation records, and function-based algorithm implementations.
affected_items: Functions: Parameters, Arguments, and Return Values lesson
Functions: Local Scope and Variable Lifetime lesson
Recursion: The Call Stack and Recursive State lesson
Assessment: Function Contracts and State Management quiz
proposed_changes.generated_diff:
--- curriculum/prerequisites/functions
+++ curriculum/prerequisites/functions
@@
- prerequisite: Variables and Data Types
+ prerequisite: none
@@
! BROKEN DEPENDENCY: Parameters and arguments reference variable binding concepts.
! BROKEN DEPENDENCY: Local scope lesson assumes learners can trace variable lifetime.
! BROKEN DEPENDENCY: Recursive activation records require parameter and local-state fluency.
! BROKEN DEPENDENCY: Divide-and-conquer implementations require function-local variables.
! BROKEN DEPENDENCY: Function Contracts quiz no longer has a verified conceptual prerequisite.
